summary: Verified Resend email integration on /api/contact end-to-end. Backend now sends real emails via https://api.resend.com/emails using RESEND_API_KEY from backend/.env. POST /api/contact returns email_sent=true with a Resend message id logged, submission persisted to MongoDB, GET /api/contact returns newest-first. Validation (invalid email / missing name or message / empty name) returns 422 without crashing. Frontend contact form shows correct error toast for empty required fields and success toast 'Message sent — I'll get back to you soon.' after a valid submit (form clears). All 10 pytest cases pass.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: send_contact_email returns bool but silently swallows non-2xx Resend responses; consider surfacing a soft warning in the API response (e.g., a message field) so the UI could distinguish 'saved but not emailed' from full success. Not blocking.
server.py: RESEND_API_KEY currently checked at module load via os.environ.get. If the key is rotated, the backend needs a restart; that's expected for env vars but worth noting.

Test note: Resend is in sandbox (from onboarding@resend.dev, no verified domain) so Resend will only actually deliver messages to the account owner (dshah5744@gmail.com); other recipients would get 200 with an id but no delivery — expected per the request.
seed_data_creation: Test rows are created with TEST_ prefixed names/emails via POST /api/contact; not cleaned up automatically. Roughly 5 test emails were sent to dshah5744@gmail.com during this run (below the 'excessive' threshold).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Resend integration is live and working. Backend logs at /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log show 'Resend email sent: <id>' entries for every successful send. RESEND_API_KEY, EMAIL_FROM_ADDRESS=onboarding@resend.dev, CONTACT_RECIPIENT=dshah5744@gmail.com are in backend/.env. When testing again, limit to 1-2 successful sends. Test asserts email_sent is True (was False in earlier iterations before the key was configured). Use REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env for all requests.
verified_assertions: GET /api/contact contains newly created id and returns list sorted newest-first
Backend log: 'Resend email sent: <uuid>' after each successful POST
Frontend empty-fields submit → error toast 'Please fill in your name, email and message.'
Frontend valid submit → success toast 'Message sent — I'll get back to you soon.' and form fields cleared
GET /api/ returns Hello World; /api/status POST+GET regression passes
